new_subsections.title: §1.6.4 — Progression structure: the sequential per-tier model + the three tracks
new_subsections.purpose: Formalize the V2 progression spine in one load-bearing place that §1.5.1, §1.6.3 and §1.12 reference. Define: (1) SEQUENTIAL per-tier — Phase 1 climbs the rungs via CURATED per-rung activities tracked by a per-rung-reset NUMERIC rung-meter (a real §4 curve, ≥30-min floor back-solved) AND story milestones (AND-gate); Phase 2 = after the final rung, the estate-influence/pillar grind unlocks → the hybrid gate tiers up; pillar DEEDS gated to phase 2. (2) The THREE clean combat tracks (kills/combat-XP→character level + HP/attrs; recognised deeds→Arms pillar; per-rung curated activities→the Combat Rank rung-meter) — stating what one kill writes to each. (3) The hybrid good/great/excellent profile (Q7) as the phase-2 tier-gate. Note exact curves/thresholds deferred to §4.

Read in full: prd_human_feedback.md Blocks L (Q1-Q56 + PD-1) and M (FU1-FU23), the §1 PRD section (lines 46-834), and the two master sheets (brainstorms/2026-06-26-prd-decisions-master.md and brainstorms/2026-06-26-prd-v2-followups.md) for framing. Two cross-cutting tensions the rewrite must resolve consistently in §1:
(1) NAMING — Q9 (Block L) renames the Arms rank-gate 'Combat Standing' → 'Combat Rank'; FU14 (Block M, newer) still loosely writes 'Combat-Standing rung-meter' for the same track. Q9 is the explicit naming decision, so standardize on 'Combat Rank' for the martial rung-gate and its rung-meter; treat FU14's wording as informal. This is load-bearing (NEEDS ADR) because 'Combat Standing' appears in §1.5.1, §1.5.4, §1.12, §1.13.
(2) DEEDS PHASING — FU7 gates pillar DEEDS to phase 2 (post-final-rung), but the V1 T0 ladder table currently accrues Arms deeds at R5/R6. The rewrite must move pillar-deed accrual out of phase 1 (the rungs) into phase 2 in BOTH §1.5.1 (table) and §1.12 (reveals). The per-rung curated COMBAT activities still feed the Combat Rank rung-meter during phase 1 — only the Arms PILLAR is deferred. This is the single most error-prone consistency point.

The biggest single rewrite is §1.6.3 (tier-gating: simple thresholds → hybrid good/great/excellent + sequential phasing) — recommend authoring the new §1.6.4 first, then pointing §1.6.3, §1.5.1, and §1.12 at it to avoid three divergent descriptions of the same model.

Decisions confirmed as NOT touching §1 (owned by other sections, noted only as cross-refs in §1.14 D-013): the save-layer internals (FU1/FU2/FU3/Q44/Q45/Q46), RNG cursor mechanics (Q2/Q3), Math.pow ban (Q36), verifier hardening (Q28), a11y/font/license/credits/content-rating internals (Q18/Q48/Q52/Q51/Q53/Q54), durability/satiety formulas (Q33/FU17/Q31/FU16 — referenced only as guardrails), intra-line dialogue data model (Q34/FU22), GameState shape (FU5), and the fun-proxy instrumentation (Q4/FU9/Q20-cadence). 39 distinct Q/FU ids drive §1 changes.

Combat uses a SEPARATE satietyRate coefficient from the labour floor (tune independently). satietyMax = base + per-(combat-)level growth. Never a hard wall.

State exactly what one kill writes (level only — never the rung-meter, never Arms directly).

Combat is INCREMENTAL: T0 starts with EXACTLY ONE weapon. Rewrite the §2.8(e) R3 dump ("drill yard + Combat panel + idle-combat all at once") into the staggered ladder, one reveal per beat: R3 = single starter weapon + bare auto-resolve loop + retreat → durability bands at R4 (with the loot→craft loop) → stance slot at R5 → ability/item slots at the first weapon-line L10 milestone → 2nd weapon line at T1 → 3rd at T2.

Add per-WEAPON archetype params (baseSpeed / reach / targetCount / attack profile) + a signature ability — distinct identity now lives on the weapon, not only on Stance. The crude carrying-pole is a 0th IMPROVISED weapon (not a line). Sketch CombatEncounterState (added additively at its M2/M5 milestone, not pre-declared in M0 per FU5).

Weapon attackPower scaled by 4 GRADED durability bands (75+/50+/1+/0 → 1.0/0.9/0.75/0.55), FIXED wear per FIGHT (cheap, replay-stable). NEVER auto-unequip — a weapon stays equipped & functional even at 0 (the 0.55 floor); never weaponless. Armour bands identically on defense.

Add a satietyRate multiplier on attackPower (lighter on attackSpeed), flat above ~0.7 satietyMax → ~0.5 floor; a SEPARATE combat coefficient. "Adequate satiety" = ≥~0.7, where the LOCKED 20-35% first-fight win-rate is re-measured (re-spec it "at adequate satiety"); bound the throttle so the floor only costs a few win-rate points (never below ~15%).

Retreat = a CLEAN escape valve: keep HP + loot, modest clock cost, NEVER dents Influence. Abandoning a DEFEND deed = a failed defend (a small recoverable Arms dent). Add headless win-rate bands at R3/V2/V5/G1/G5 as a 2nd pacing proxy (numbers tune-later, FU19).

Keep conditioning as the ZERO-stat, one-way labour-built enablement gate (the weak→capable gate), but note it is now the EXCEPTION: other labour skills feed small combat texture via per-skill perks (§2.7). Conditioning grants ZERO combat stat / training-rate bonus.

FLAG a naming conflict for the rewrite to resolve: Q9 wanted the Arms rank-gate renamed "Combat Standing" → "Combat Rank" (so "Standing" = 官威 only); the newer FU14 still calls the per-rung meter the "Combat-Standing rung-meter." Newest-block-wins → keep "Combat Standing" for the rung-meter OR apply "Combat Rank"; pick ONE term and apply project-wide.

Add an explicit per-mob `level` field on MobDef (hand-tunable; defaults ~ the dangerRing's expected character-level) feeding the on-kill combat-XP path. Move the BeliefBeast registry to content/beliefBeasts.ts (separate from grindable mobs).

Durability = GRADED bands (4 bands on weapon attackPower; armour bands on defense), NEVER auto-unequip, repair/re-craft restores to max, FIXED wear per fight. EquipDef weapons carry archetype params (baseSpeed/reach/targetCount) + signature ability; the weapon roster GROWS (~9-10 across v1; T0 +2 / T1 +3 / T2 +4). Durability bands reveal at R4 with the loot→craft loop.

Crafting reveals as a TOP-LEVEL nav tab (Q10). The market-saturation damper applies progressively per-unit on bulk sales (Q42). Repair/re-craft restores weapon/armour durability to max (FU17). Trade/coin numbers (incl. the silk meibutsu economics) deferred to M4 (Q13).

Intra-line dialogue branching IS in v1: a flat choices[] list per node (choices[] + ChoiceId); a choice's effect = locksLineIds[]/flags. DATA, not scripting; deterministic (no RNG); only chosen-flags persist (save-light). Authored in content/dialogue.ts. Add choices[]/ChoiceId to the TextLine data shape.

REMOVE the fixed "~4 v1 quest types" cap and the "Escort/patrol/... parked for T2+" budget framing. Author whatever quests fit each stage; the 4 grind types (PEST CONTROL/HUNT/CLEAR/DEFEND) are just the T0 STARTING set, not a cap; more/interesting quests welcome, especially later tiers (supersedes D-012's "lean 4"). Drop the "parked" cap from the Quest type union.

DEFEND remains the canonical combat Arms standing-earner. Clarify that some quest/deed completions are the per-rung CURATED activities feeding the Combat-Standing rung-meter in PHASE 1, while pillar DEEDS accrue in PHASE 2 (FU7). Quest log = a top-level tab (Q10).

BeliefBeast registry → content/beliefBeasts.ts (Q3). Co-locate the single residual-ambiguity boundary-jizō at the weir/ford (Q11 — one find-spot; "presumed dead → back from the dead"). Rumours stay design-staggered/organic per-tier (aligns with FU4, no all-at-once dump).

Weather hazards & festivals carry MECHANICAL effects, bounded ±10% on labour/combat rates (+ festival economic beats). Weather/lunar DERIVED from the day-keyed RNG sub-stream (deriveDayKeyed), not stored. World-sim content (SeasonRules / Festival / WeatherHazard) authored in content/world.ts (add content/festivals.ts row), generated/verified like the other registries.

SEQUENTIAL per-tier (FU7): PHASE 1 = climb the rungs (R0→R7) via CURATED per-rung activities (1-to-many designed activities per rung, NOT a single repeat-counter) tracked by the rung-meter + story; PHASE 2 = the estate-influence/pillar grind UNLOCKS after the FINAL rung → tier-up. Pillar DEEDS are gated to phase 2 (not accruing across rungs — prevents "half the rungs, maxed deeds"). Rung promotion = AND-gate (numeric rung-meter ≥ threshold AND story flags; surface "awaiting X" when one lags). Revise "capstone confirms the tier": the capstone rung confirms phase 1; the phase-2 hybrid pillar gate is the actual tier-gate.

Both meters (EstateService, Combat-Standing) are NUMERIC and PER-RUNG-RESET; each rung's threshold = (≥30-min floor × that rung's eligible-activity rate), back-solved like the koku column so meter and floor stay in lockstep. Combat-Standing rung-meter is fed by per-rung curated activities, NOT raw kills/XP (FU14). Add rungActivityTags tagging which activities advance which rung; double-counting allowed but each stream sums independently.

Rescope the "ZERO mechanical bonus": returning MEMORY (the backstory reveal) grants NO retroactive stat/recipe bonus, but the present-day relationship pride buff (modest global skill-XP) + the ~10-15% time-to-tier speedup STAY (Q12). Reclaiming the name "Tahei" = the Origin O5 capstone — EARNED and MISSABLE (Origin-gated, Q5); the Otsuru/Tama TRUTH stays spine-guaranteed at G6 (§5). Add Nihonbashi to the fictional allow-list.

Conditioning stays the ZERO-stat one-way enablement gate on combat rungs; note that OTHER labour skills now grant small per-skill perks (Q6/FU8) — conditioning alone grants zero combat stat/training-rate bonus.

REPLACE "PER-TIER REQUIRED PILLARS via SIMPLE THRESHOLDS ... NO floor/overflow (formula REJECTED)" with the HYBRID gate: per-pillar-per-tier thresholds — GOOD in ALL revealed pillars · GREAT in 2-3 · EXCELLENT in 1-2. Semantics: good = expected baseline · great = really strong · excellent = above-and-beyond. Per-tier revealed-pillar set: T0 = 2 (Arms+Estate) SPECIAL CASE ("good in both + excellent in 1", no phantom 3rd), T1 = 3, T2 = 3. NO overflow-substitution (floor only). Trade ≤⅓ stays the one hard structural cap. Rewrite the table's "Required pillars (simple thresholds)" column to the good/great/excellent distribution per tier.

The Q7 hybrid pillar distribution is a SEPARATE phase-2, capstone-only tier-gate, ANDed with the capstone rung-meter+story (FU11); pillar deeds gated to phase 2 (FU7). Rewrite the TierGate data shape: carry per-pillar good/great/excellent bands + a DISTRIBUTION predicate (not a single requiredPillarThresholds map; delete "no floor/overflow fields exist" line — replace with the distribution semantics).

Add the below-high-water seasonal RESTORE branch for dents: a small seasonal restore lifts a dented pillar back toward its untouched high-water WITHOUT advancing the high-water ("self-heals, never a wipe"). Keep the two-shape accrual (achievement jumps + judged results, new-high-water-only) + per-event caps.

Add BROADER cross-pillar combos as the T2 anti-slump device (multiple pillar pairs / larger magnitude). Computed AFTER the trade-≤⅓ clamp; counted INSIDE the deeds budget + per-event cap; EXCLUDED from the gate-threshold check (a combo can NEVER substitute for being "good in ALL"); trade-≤⅓ stays HARD (verifier-proven it can't breach ⅓ via a combo). A narrow §4.3 no-leakage exception.
Pillar bars show DISTANCE-TO-NEXT-GATE; number-flash uses the §2 gain/loss tokens (gain=--ai, loss=--beni); vermilion reserved for rank-up/seal beats.
Update the T3 "stub in v1" row: v1 ends on the castle-town / Daikan FIRST-CONTACT (magistrate's office, stone walls) — drop the porter-guild framing; rewrite the stale T3 header (detail in §5).

Author E3 for v1: estate grows E0→E3 (un-park E3 "Prosperous" + its §4.7.5 cost row). EstateStage id (E0…E3 in v1); E4–E5 parked. E3 authored in M5 as a koku/Arms sink folded into the G-rungs (build/authoring cost only — play-time budget is a FLOOR per FU18). Update the "E0–E2" references throughout the subsection.

REPLACE "Canon: IndexedDB ... single autosave; localStorage note superseded" with MULTI-BACKEND redundant save: IndexedDB primary + localStorage + sessionStorage. ATOMIC write (one put, never clear-then-rewrite). On save, write all available backends; on load, read ALL backends and pick NEWEST by a monotonic SAVE-COUNTER (the real selector) + a save-layer timestamp tiebreaker (a documented core-lint exemption — metadata, NOT game logic; the deterministic core stays clock-free). Calm "couldn't save — export a backup" notice on any rejection. Survives itch cross-origin-iframe partition/eviction. Built FULL in M0 (FU1).

Save schema is BACKWARDS-COMPATIBLE / ADDITIVE (protobuf/thrift-style: new fields optional with defaults, never remove/repurpose) → migrations become rare; keep raw-backup + rollback + a future-version guard as the safety net (never load a forward-version backend — fall through to newest readable, then reject-to-recovery). Add an app-identity MAGIC field (app:'kami-kakushi') + reject-to-recovery on a bad/foreign id.

RNG = { seed, cursors:{combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen} } (per-named-stream monotonic cursors, NOT a single counter). Persist market-saturation ONLY (weather/lunar are DERIVED, not stored). Nest estateWealth.subEngines{land,treasury,trade} (storage for the trade-≤⅓ clamp + per-strand high-water). M0 skeleton = {hp,satiety,attributePoints} + character.level(=1) + satietyMax-at-floor; do NOT pre-declare subEngines/CombatEncounterState/dialogue choices — add additively at their milestone.

Add explicit CONFIRM on destructive actions (import / fresh-start, plus the genuinely-unrecoverable rare actions: no-respec attribute allocation, rare-material consume, narrative-route choices) + an auto PRE-OVERWRITE snapshot. Note Math.pow is banned (integer-pow) so exported saves replay byte-identically cross-engine.

Add verifier checks: gate-monotonicity & ceiling (no rung needs more than its tier can grant), accrual tie-out (deeds sum to the gate share within tolerance), real-name DENYLIST lint, trade-can-never-breach-⅓-via-combo proof, each combat track sums INDEPENDENTLY. FLIP the no-hidden-edge check from "labour→combat == 0" to "each per-skill perk is SMALL-magnitude (not zero, not a global ≤CAP)" with conditioning still asserted ZERO. Add the §6.1 Math.pow/exp/log/trig lint (whitelist sqrt).
Tag duplicated derived values "illustrative — see generated"; align gen-docs paths (docs/balance/ + docs/content/); the world-sim content/world.ts registry is generated/verified like the others.

Load-bearing period motifs (pillar / season / rarity marks) = INLINE SVG (consistent across OSes); emoji COSMETIC-only. This corrects the "no asset pipeline" claim (one acknowledged small motif+audio asset set).

"GOOD audio": mixed SYNTHESIZED Web Audio + original/CC0 samples (the ONE acknowledged small curated audio set); correct the register to "text + emoji + CSS + a small curated audio set"; mute toggle stays. Licensing/credits surfaced (s2-50).

Do the low-cost a11y correctness items now: a persistent quiet a11y entry point from minute one; an ARIA live-region scoped to narration+milestone ("polite"); a large-textScale reflow case + a screen-reader acceptance pass. Functional/hint text → --ink-soft (passes WCAG AA on all paper surfaces); --ink-faint decorative-only; darken the meter fill for contrast. Extend A11ySettings with live-region scope.
Self-host the OFL fonts (kill Google dynamic-subsetting — it breaks offline + the itch relative-base); bundle the OFL license; clear the Reserved-Font-Name rule.

Update the flagged items to V2: item 1 (simple-threshold gate → HYBRID good/great/excellent, no overflow, trade ≤⅓ still hard); item 6 (lean skill/quest set → exactly ONE weapon at T0 + growing roster ~9-10 + per-skill perks + NO fixed quest-type budget). Add new flagged items: the THREE combat tracks; the SEQUENTIAL phase-1/phase-2 progression; the MULTI-BACKEND save layer.
Macronize gōshi / rōnin project-wide; confirm 官威 (kan'i) = Standing & Office. Resolve the "Combat Standing" vs "Combat Rank" overload (see s2-20).

new_subsections.title: The three combat tracks
new_subsections.purpose: Crisply tabulate the three INDEPENDENT combat-fed tracks and what one kill / deed / rung-activity writes to each: (1) kills + combat-XP → character level (HP + attribute points, Q1); (2) recognized DEEDS → the Arms pillar (the influence economy); (3) per-rung curated activities → the Combat-Standing rung-meter (§2.15). Replaces the deleted conflated CombatDeedsPool.

new_subsections.title: Combat-reveal ladder (incremental)
new_subsections.where: new subsection inside §2.8 (after the new three-tracks subsection)
new_subsections.purpose: Enumerate the staggered, one-per-beat combat reveal order with the trigger kind per step: R3 starter weapon + auto-resolve loop + retreat (combat rung) → R4 durability bands (loot→craft) → R5 stance slot → weapon-line L10 ability/item slots (weapon-skill milestone) → 2nd line T1 / 3rd line T2 (combat rung), signatures at L25/L50. Kills the R3 UI-dump.

new_subsections.title: Weapon roster (incremental, ~9-10 across v1)
new_subsections.purpose: List the growing period-appropriate weapon roster (T0 starts with 1; unlocks T0 +2 / T1 +3 / T2 +4) with each weapon's archetype params (baseSpeed / reach / targetCount / attack profile) + a signature ability; crude pole = a 0th improvised weapon, not a line. Replaces "2-3 weapon lines at T0".

new_subsections.title: Per-skill perks — bounded labour→combat cross-feed
new_subsections.purpose: Specify the per-skill perk/flat-bonus track (~2-8 perks or ~3-8 small flat bonuses per skill, unlocked by leveling it), the SEPARATE skillCombatBonus channel (not attribute, not level), stackable with NO hard global cap (bounded by small magnitude + incremental skill unlock + holistic scaling, risk accepted), and conditioning as the zero-stat exception. Replaces the §2.7/§4.5.3 "== 0 no-cross-feed" wall.

new_subsections.title: Sequential per-tier progression — rungs (phase 1) → pillar grind (phase 2)
new_subsections.where: new subsection bridging §2.15 / §2.16 (e.g. lead-in to §2.16, or new §2.15 closing subsection)
new_subsections.purpose: Author the two-phase model: Phase 1 climb the rungs via curated per-rung activities + the numeric per-rung-reset rung-meter + story (AND-gate); Phase 2 the estate-influence/pillar grind unlocks after the final rung and the Q7 hybrid pillar distribution is the real tier-gate. Pillar deeds gated to phase 2. Defines the rung-meter accrual law (threshold = ≥30-min floor × eligible-activity rate).

new_subsections.title: Hybrid tier-gate — good / great / excellent
new_subsections.where: new subsection inside §2.16 (replacing the simple-threshold gate paragraph)
new_subsections.purpose: State the distribution rule (good in ALL revealed pillars, great in 2-3, excellent in 1-2), the semantics (baseline / really-strong / above-and-beyond), the per-tier revealed-pillar set (T0=2 special case, T1=3, T2=3), no overflow-substitution, and the per-pillar-per-tier threshold overhaul against the fixed deed inventory.

new_subsections.title: Multi-backend save layer
new_subsections.purpose: Specify the backend abstraction (IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage), atomic write, app-identity magic field, the monotonic save-counter newest-wins selector (+ save-layer timestamp tiebreaker as a documented core-lint exemption), reject-to-recovery, additive schema, and itch cross-origin-iframe survival. Built full in M0.

new_subsections.title: About / Credits surface
new_subsections.where: new subsection inside §2.21 (or a small §2.20 sibling)
new_subsections.purpose: Define a small About/Credits surface: authorship, the commit-SHA build stamp, font/audio attributions, clean-room attestation; note the license split (permissive code MIT/Apache-2.0 + reserved game content) and itch content descriptors as the deploy-checklist link (detail in §7).

The ~28.5h budget is now a FLOOR, not a target/ceiling (FU18): the saga can/should be LONGER — a long OSRS-rough grind, interleaved, never brick-walled; §4.8 becomes a MINIMUM-grind model. The 4.5/8/16h tier figures and the ≥30-min/rung floor are minimums, not caps. Annotates D-016.

T3 row already reads 'Castle-town (stub)' per Q24 — keep.

Author 'Progression architecture — three tracks, two phases'. THREE independent tracks (FU14): (1) character level ← combat XP/kills only → HP + attribute points (Q1); (2) the rung-meter ← per-rung CURATED activities (a separate authored set, NOT pillar deeds, NOT raw kills); (3) the four influence pillars ← deeds + seasonal. TWO phases per tier (FU7): Phase 1 = climb rungs R0→R7 via rung-meter + story (+ curated activities + throughput); Phase 2 = the estate-influence/pillar grind unlocks AFTER the final rung → the hybrid tier-gate → tier-up. Pillar DEEDS are gated to Phase 2 (prevents 'half the rungs, maxed deeds').

Retitle §4.1 from 'SIMPLE bands — NO floor/overflow' to 'HYBRID good/great/excellent tier-gate (the Phase-2 tier-gate)'. New shape: GOOD in ALL revealed pillars (breadth floor — reverses the old 'no global floor'), GREAT in 2–3, EXCELLENT in 1–2; NO overflow/substitution (this half of the old rule survives). Per-tier revealed-pillar set: T0 = 2 (Arms+Estate) special case = good in both + excellent in 1, no phantom 3rd; T1 = 3 (+Office); T2 = 3. Semantics: good = expected baseline, great = really strong, excellent = above-and-beyond. This is the Phase-2 tier-gate (separate from the per-rung rung-meter+story gate, FU11).

Replace the single-threshold-per-pillar table with a per-pillar-per-tier GOOD/GREAT/EXCELLENT triple table, re-derived against the fixed deed inventory — NOT simple 0.2×/0.5× ratios (FU10 explicitly rejects ratios). Map the old named threshold to roughly the gate reference; author the triples (T0–T2 ship; T3/T4 stub) in balance.ts. Only the first 3 tiers are load-bearing for v1.

Rewrite the 'why magnitudes survived', 'reading the drift', and levers prose to the hybrid model and the Phase-1/Phase-2 split: the rung-meter+story gate the rungs; this hybrid pillar distribution is the Phase-2 tier-gate. Drift (Arms+Estate early → Office+Name late) still holds. Levers = the good/great/excellent triples + the great/excellent counts.

Author 'The rung-meter accrual law' (FU6): per-rung-RESET meter; each rung's threshold = (≥30-min floor × that rung's eligible curated-activity rate), back-solved exactly like the §4.8.1 koku column so meter and time floor stay in lockstep; AND-gate — promote only when meter ≥ threshold AND all story flags set, surfacing 'awaiting X' when one side lags (FU6/Q30). Fed by the per-rung CURATED activity set (separate from the pillar-deed inventory — FU7). Rename the combat rung-meter 'Combat Standing' → 'Combat Rank' (Q9; 'Standing' reserved for the 官威 Standing & Office pillar); 'Estate Service' meter name unchanged.

Reframe: pillar accrual (deeds + seasonal) is the PHASE-2 grind (FU7), not a per-rung accrual. Keep the 70/30 deeds/seasonal split (LOCKED). Add a forward-reference to the T2 cross-pillar combos exception (§4.3.1, FU20).

Four interlocking edits: (a) deeds accrue in PHASE 2 only (FU7) — recompute the cadence claim, which is no longer 'one act every ~4.5–5 min across the whole tier' but tier-relative within the phase-2 window (Q20: T0~5/T1~8/T2~13 min); (b) the 70%-of-gate itemizations must re-sum against the new HYBRID gate (good/great/excellent), not the old single threshold (FU10); (c) Q29: re-itemize T0 Estate (~560 ip) with LAND/TREASURY deeds ONLY — DELETE the 6 trade contracts (trade strand opens at T1, village shops = first market); (d) Q23: more/interesting quests welcome (esp. later tiers) — the inventory may grow beyond the 'fixed' set, but every gate still ties out.

Note that Q29's instruction to 'pull Arms-deed onset to R4 so R6/E2 clears on deeds' is SUPERSEDED by FU7 — all pillar deeds (Arms included) are gated to Phase 2, so there is no per-rung Arms-deed onset to fix. Remove the per-rung Arms-deed onset framing.

Reconcile with FU7/FU10: (a) the seasonal stream is part of the PHASE-2 pillar grind — clarify the pillar high-water appraisal toward the tier-gate runs during Phase 2 (seasons still tick in Phase 1 for weather/festival flavour but don't accrue pillars); reconcile the 8-season tie-out (which assumed seasons spread across the whole tier) with the phase-2 window; (b) re-derive JUDGE_K = SEASONAL_SHARE·(gate reference) against the new HYBRID gate magnitudes rather than the old single threshold. Keep the high-water-mark rule, sqrt shaping, TIER_REF normalizers, and 30% share.

Add a reference that the per-season hook also runs the Q32 below-high-water dent-restore branch (a small seasonal restore lifting a dented value toward its untouched high-water WITHOUT advancing high-water).

KEEP the ≤⅓ HARD invariant verbatim. Add two clarifications: T0 has NO trade strand (Q29 — opens at T1); and FU20 cross-pillar combos are computed AFTER the trade clamp and can never breach ⅓ (verifier-proven, §6.6).

Add the Q32 explicit below-high-water restore branch: a small seasonal restore regrows a dented `value` toward its untouched `highWater` without advancing highWater ('self-heals within ~1–2 seasons, never a wipe'). Keep DENT_FRACTION=0.10, scripted-only, never touches highWater.

Three edits: (a) unify the `combatLevel` term to the single combat-fed CHARACTER level (Q1/FU14); (b) the 'no cross-pillar leakage except roll-ups' rule gains a narrow, broadened T2 exception — cross-pillar combos (Q22/FU20, see §4.3.1); (c) note the labour-skill combat bonus (FU8) is a SEPARATE additive channel (§4.5.4), NOT a §4.3 conversion weight — §4.3 still routes deeds→pillars with no skill/level→Influence-direct edge.

Author 'Cross-pillar combos (T2 anti-slump exception)' (Q22/FU20): BROADER than the lean proposal — multiple pillar pairs / larger magnitude. State which pairs combo, the magnitude, and the proof they don't breach invariants: computed AFTER the trade clamp, kept below trade-≤⅓, EXCLUDED from the hybrid gate-threshold check (a combo can NEVER satisfy 'good in ALL'), bounded within (or as a rebate on) the 70/30 + 0.04 envelope; §6.6 verifier-proven. Pairs with the seasonal-reward-rotation as the dual T2 anti-slump device.

Clarify the cross-feed wall: the no-labour→combat-VIA-ATTRIBUTES wall STAYS (attributes remain combat-fed only; labour skills never grant STR/AGI), BUT a bounded labour→combat channel now exists via per-skill perks (a separate `skillCombatBonus` addend, never an attribute — §4.5.4, Q6/FU8). Unify the level term: the combat-fed CHARACTER level grants +1 attr point / 2 levels + HP (Q1/FU14). Keep no-respec-in-v1 LOCKED.

FLIP the '==0 / labour skill can NEVER grant a combat-feed attribute' type-wall into the bounded per-skill-perk model (FU8): every skill (labour + combat) has a perk / flat-bonus track (~2–8 perks each) unlocked by leveling it; labour-skill perks MAY include a small `skillCombatBonus` (a separate additive term — NOT an attribute, NOT character level). Stackable, NO hard global cap; bounded by small per-perk magnitudes + incremental skill unlock + holistic balance (gear/level/attrs/enemy-scaling grow together); risk accepted. Conditioning stays the ZERO-stat enablement gate; big combat power stays combat-only (Q1).

Author 'Per-skill perk tracks (the bounded labour→combat channel)' (FU8): the data model — each skill carries a perk track; labour perks may grant the small `skillCombatBonus`; magnitudes/cadence (perks unlocked by leveling) as balance.ts levers. The §6.6 verifier flips from asserting the cross-feed edge '== 0' to asserting each perk magnitude is small/bounded (no global ≤CAP, per-perk smallness) — Q28 alignment.

Reconcile the L10 '+1 attribute (combat skills only)' row and the milestone-choice no-respec box with the new perk model: labour-skill milestones may now grant a bounded combat perk (via skillCombatBonus, not an attribute); milestone-choice no-respec rule unchanged (LOCKED).

Augment effective stats: attackPower = (weaponBase + 1.2·STR + gearAtk + skillBonus + skillCombatBonus) · satietyRate · durabilityBand; defense gains an armour durabilityBand multiplier. Add satietyRate (FU16), skillCombatBonus (FU8), durabilityBand (FU17) terms. Add the missing satietyMax formula = base + per-level growth off the combat-fed level (Q47/FU21). Unify characterLevel = the combat-fed character level (Q1/FU14).

Author the satiety→combat throttle (FU16/Q31): a `satietyRate` multiplier on attackPower (a lighter one on attackSpeed), flat above ~0.7·satietyMax → knee to a ~0.5 floor; a SEPARATE combat coefficient from the labour STAMINA_RATE_FLOOR (tune independently); 'adequate satiety' = ≥~0.7, the reference at which the LOCKED 20–35% first-fight win-rate is now measured; bounded so the floor never pushes win-rate below ~15%. Satiety throttles combat ('eat before you fight').

Author graded durability bands (Q33/FU17): 4 integer bands on weapon attackPower (75+/50+/1+/0 → ×1.0/0.9/0.75/0.55); FIXED wear per FIGHT (cheap ticks, replay-stable — not per swing); armour bands identically on defense; repair/re-craft restores to max; NEVER auto-unequip / never weaponless (it's an auto-battler). All thresholds/multipliers in balance.ts.

Clean THREE-track separation (FU14): one kill writes (1) combat XP → CHARACTER level (HP + attr points) via mobLevel·COMBAT_XP_K; (2) weapon-skill XP → the equipped weapon line. It does NOT bump the rung-meter (per-rung curated activities do) and does NOT accrue a pillar deed EXCEPT a *recognized* clear/defend → Arms (Phase 2). mobLevel = an explicit per-mob `MobDef.level` field, defaulting ~ the dangerRing's expected character-level (FU15). Rewrite to stop conflating level with any deeds pool.

(a) First-fight win-rate 20–35% now tagged 'at adequate satiety (≥~0.7)' (Q31/FU16); (b) the 'no labour conditioning bonus enters these numbers' line: conditioning stays the zero-stat gate, but a small bounded skillCombatBonus now exists (FU8) — first-fight numbers measured without heavy perk stacking; (c) keep the soft-setback shape LOCKED; (d) cross-ref the new win-rate-bands sub.

Author 'Win-rate bands (the 2nd pacing proxy)' (Q16/FU19): target headless win-rates at R3/V2/V5/G1/G5 — fresh-at-rung humbling (~30–45%, R3's 20–35% the anchor) and comfortable after that rung's intended training/gear (~80%+), tightening slightly per tier; wired into the M3/M6 fun-proxy harness alongside the pacing floor. Tune-later magnitudes.

Author retreat resolution (Q16): retreat = clean escape — keep HP/loot, modest clock cost, NEVER dents Influence; abandoning a DEFEND/defence-deed counts as a failed defend (the only Influence movement on a loss is the scripted Arms dent, §4.2.4).

maps.notes:
Precedence applied: newest-block-wins (Block M / FU* supersedes Block L / Q*, both supersede A–K). Per-decision detail cross-checked against brainstorms/2026-06-26-prd-decisions-master.md and brainstorms/2026-06-26-prd-v2-followups.md. Source file: /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/prd.md, §5 = lines 3102-3888.

§5 owns NARRATIVE SHAPE only — numbers (rung-meter curve, character-level XP curve, hybrid gate thresholds, E3 cost, durability/satiety/combo terms) stay in §4; rung copy + the combat-reveal ladder stay in §3. The §5 changes are the story-shape reflections of those decisions.

Cross-section TERMINOLOGY tension to resolve in the rewrite (flag to §2/§3 owners): Q9 (Block L) renames the Arms rank-gate 'Combat Standing' -> 'Combat Rank' so 'Standing' = the 官威/kan'i pillar only; but FU14 (Block M, newer) names the per-rung combat meter the 'Combat-Standing rung-meter'. §5 does NOT use either term literally today (it says 'Arms deeds/standing'), so s5-5/s5-10 should adopt whichever reconciled term §2/§3 settle on. Newest-block-wins nominally favours FU14's 'Combat Standing' for the rung-meter, but the §2/§3 rewrite must reconcile the two so 'Standing' isn't overloaded again.

needs_adr=true items are the load-bearing reversals materially expressed in §5: s5-3/s5-9/s5-19 (FU7 phase-1/phase-2 reshape of the locked transition-gate narration) + s5-5 (FU14 deeds-gating/track-separation), s5-18 (Q8 un-parks the locked E2-cap), s5-20 + s5-30 (Q5 restructures the locked truth/name climax — the game's emotional heart, a ⚠ your-call decision), s5-25 (Q24 swaps the locked T3 stub node). These all flow from the governing D-022 'V2 decisions supersede prior canon/locks' rule; annotate prior canon (canon §I for the stub, D-016 for E2-cap, the old guaranteed-G6-name line) rather than deleting.

Already-correct (no change): Kuzuhara no-personal-tie (s5-23); the T0 villain-less debt arc (s5-8); conditioning as the zero-stat gate (s5-35); Otsuru/Osen rename (Items-flagged #3, already resolved).

changes.what: Update the one-line save-model description from 'IndexedDB + base64, versioned minimal-state, migrations' to 'MULTI-BACKEND redundant save (IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage), atomic write, app-identity magic field, additive backwards-compatible schema, monotonic-counter newest-wins selector'. Also add to the §6 scope list: the three combat tracks, intra-line dialogue data model, and the bounded per-skill cross-feed.

§6 is no longer ONLY an 'elaboration of D-013'. Note it now also IMPLEMENTS the V2 decisions under D-022 (governing, newest-wins): multi-backend save (Q37), additive schema (Q45), RNG per-named-stream cursors (Q2), Math.pow ban (Q36), the bounded labour->combat verifier flip (Q6), the feature-rich data model incl. intra-line dialogue & subEngines (Q34), and the no-runtime-reveal-queue design rule (FU4). These are real reversals/new locks, not just buildable detail.

Add a new lint rule: BAN Math.pow / Math.exp / Math.log / trig in core (whitelist Math.sqrt); all growth-curve powers use integer-power-by-repeated-multiplication. Rationale: byte-identical cross-engine replay + portable exported saves (kills the 1-ULP hole).

Keep Date.now/performance.now BANNED in src/core/, but document an explicit SAVE-LAYER exemption: persistence/ may call Date.now for the save-layer timestamp used only as the newest-wins TIEBREAKER (metadata, not game logic) — the deterministic core stays clock-free. Name this as a recorded core-lint exemption.

Bundled-assets note that CORRECTS the 'no asset pipeline' claim: self-host the SIL OFL fonts (no Google dynamic subsetting; bundle the OFL license; works offline + with itch relative-base); a small curated audio set (synthesized Web Audio + original/CC0 samples) bundled into dist/; inline SVG for load-bearing period motifs. Build injects a commit-SHA build stamp.

Rewrite to: persisted per-named-stream cursors `{ seed, cursors:{combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen} }` (NOT a single counter, NOT child-RNG-by-splitting); plus a pure stateless day-keyed helper `deriveDayKeyed(seed,'weather',day)` for weather/lunar that is NOT a persisted cursor.

Replace 'simple per-tier required-pillar thresholds (no floor/overflow formula)' with: HYBRID good/great/excellent per-pillar-per-tier gate (good in ALL revealed pillars, great in 2-3, excellent in 1-2; T0 2-pillar special; NO overflow); pillar DEEDS gated to phase-2 (FU7); nested estateWealth.subEngines{land,treasury,trade} clamp; cross-pillar combos computed POST trade-clamp.

Add: each skill (labour + combat) carries a per-skill PERKS track (~2-8 perks / small flat combat bonuses, unlocked by leveling it). This is the bounded labour->combat channel; the real bound is incremental skill unlock (skills reveal per rung/tier), not a global cap. Conditioning stays the ZERO-stat enablement gate.

Expand: combat-XP -> character.level track (Q1); per-mob MobDef.level (FU15); graded durability bands on attackPower/defense (FU17); satiety throttle satietyRate on attackPower (FU16); clean retreat (Q16); the staggered reveal of stance/ability/item slots (FU12); a bigger weapon roster with archetype params + signature ability (FU13); CombatEncounterState save-resume (Q34).

Add: reveal staggering is a DESIGN property of the authored unlock schedule (one-at-a-time by construction) — there is NO stored runtime reveal-queue. Genuine multi-element single-feature reveals are bespoke one-offs designed per case.

Note the app tick loop ACCUMULATES the fractional dtTicks remainder and hands only WHOLE-INTEGER ticks to core tick(); it also drives auto-resolve combat + auto-repeat labour while the tab is active (the 'leave it running' feel), still active-only.

Update to: multi-backend (IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage) + atomic write + app-identity magic field + monotonic-counter newest-wins selector + the additive-schema migration chain & raw-backup. Still side-effectful, never imported BY core.

Rename to `content/dialogue.ts`; add `choices[]` + `ChoiceId` with `locksLineIds[]`/flags effects — intra-line branching as flat DATA (not scripting), deterministic, only chosen-flags persist.
Add a per-skill PERKS field (~2-8 perks / small flat combat bonuses, gated to skill levels); document that the verifier asserts small per-perk magnitude (not ==0, not a global cap).

Specify the bigger weapon roster (~9-10 across v1; T0 starts with 1, then +2 T0 / +3 T1 / +4 T2), each weapon carrying archetype params (baseSpeed / reach / targetCount) + a signature ability. 3 archetype lines (spear/sword/staff) anchor it.

Add per-rung CURATED activity sets (tagged by rung) that feed the rung-meter, AUTHORED SEPARATELY from the pillar-deed inventory; encode the combat-reveal ladder (R3 weapon+auto-loop+retreat -> R4 durability -> R5 stance -> weapon-L10 ability/item slots -> 2nd line T1 / 3rd T2); ranks carry rung-meter threshold + AND-gate.

Replace 'per-tier required-pillar thresholds' with the per-pillar-per-tier good/great/excellent band triples (the V2 overhaul, balanced against the fixed deed inventory); add the phase-2 deed gating and the cross-pillar combos (post-clamp, third-pillar, excluded from gate checks).
Add an explicit per-mob `level` field (MobDef.level, defaults ~ the dangerRing's expected character-level) feeding the on-kill XP path; reiterate belief-creatures live in beliefBeasts.ts not here.

Expand 'the single home for tunables' to include the new V2 curves/constants: rung-meter thresholds, good/great/excellent gate bands, per-skill perk magnitudes, durability bands (75+/50+/1+/0 -> 1.0/0.9/0.75/0.55), satiety throttle (flat >=~0.7 -> ~0.5 floor), weather ±10%, combat-XP->level curve, mobLevel defaults — all integer-pow only (no Math.pow).
Note `pillarDeltas` (deed accrual) is PHASE-2-gated (FU7) — deeds don't grant pillar value during the rung-climb. Rewards stay one bus; the dialogue choices[] effects ride the same bus.
Note NO quest-type budget: quests are authored per stage (the '4 grind types' is just the T0 starting set, not a cap); more/interesting quests welcome in later tiers.

FLIP the machine check: conditioning still grants ZERO combat stats (stays the gate), BUT each per-skill perk grants a SMALL bounded combat bonus — the verifier asserts each perk's MAGNITUDE is small (a per-perk magnitude check), NOT ==0 and NOT a single global <=CAP. Remove the absolute '==0 no-cross-feed' wall.

Add: gate-monotonicity/ceiling (no rung/tier requires more than its tier can grant); accrual tie-out (deeds sum to the gate share within tolerance); per-rung-meter monotonicity (the rung-meter target exists and is reachable from its eligible activities).

Add a real-name DENYLIST lint (flags real surnames/places — Toyama, Konoe, real daimyō/place names); maintained alongside the macron lint. Allow-list: naturalized exonyms + the invented allow-list incl. Nihonbashi.

Strengthen: trade <=1/3 of Estate & Wealth holds EVEN AFTER cross-pillar combos — combos are computed POST trade-clamp, land on a third pillar (Name), and are EXCLUDED from gate-threshold checks; the verifier proves trade can never breach 1/3 via a combo.

Add the hybrid gate-distribution check: good in ALL revealed pillars, great in 2-3, excellent in 1-2, the T0 2-pillar special case, NO overflow-substitution; and assert combos are excluded so a combo can't satisfy a required pillar.

Add gōshi / rōnin to the macron examples; add Nihonbashi to the fictional allow-list; cross-link the real-name denylist (s6-38). Note the 'Combat Standing'->'Combat Rank' rename so the lint examples track the new term.
Align gen-docs output paths to docs/balance/ + docs/content/; tag duplicated illustrative numbers 'illustrative — see generated' so hand-typed derived values can't silently drift.

Rewrite: `state.rng = { seed, cursors:{combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen} }` — per-named-stream MONOTONIC cursors (replace `{seed,counter}` and the 'derive child RNGs by splitting' model). splitmix64 stays. Add the stateless day-keyed helper `deriveDayKeyed(seed,'weather',day)` for weather/lunar (NOT a persisted cursor). tick() consumes whole-integer dtTicks; Math.random banned in core; cross-ref the Math.pow ban (§6.1).

Rewrite from 'IndexedDB single autosave' to MULTI-BACKEND redundant save: IndexedDB primary + localStorage + sessionStorage; on save, atomic write to all available backends; on load, read ALL backends and pick the newest. Survives itch cross-origin-iframe partition/eviction. Build the FULL layer in M0 (FU1).

Atomic-write rule: one atomic put per backend, never clear-then-rewrite; on ANY write rejection (quota/private-mode/torn write) show a calm persistent 'couldn't save — export a backup' banner. No silent loss.

Newest-save selector: a MONOTONIC save-counter is the real selector; a save-LAYER timestamp (documented core-lint exemption — metadata, not game logic) is the tiebreaker; NEVER load a forward-schemaVersion backend (fall through to the newest READABLE backend, then reject-to-recovery). Equal/skewed-clock handled by the counter.
App-identity magic field `app:'kami-kakushi'` for a fast reject of foreign/corrupt blobs; on a bad/stale id, reject-to-recovery (offer re-import / fresh start), never a silent half-load.

Make ADDITIVE backwards-compatible schema the PRIMARY mechanism (protobuf/thrift-style: new fields optional with defaults, never remove/repurpose) -> migrations become RARE. Keep the ordered migration chain + raw pre-migration backup + future-version guard ONLY as the safety net for unavoidable structural changes. Supersedes 'ordered forward migrations' as the default.

Update the persisted-surface list: rng CURSORS (not seed+counter), market saturation, character.level + combatXp, estateWealth.subEngines, dialogue chosen-flags, the rung-meter values — and note additive optional fields added per-milestone.

Save-safety: explicit confirm on destructive actions (import / fresh-start, and only genuinely-unrecoverable rare actions) + an automatic pre-overwrite snapshot (extends the multi-backend write) before any overwrite.
Keep base64 export/import as the manual backstop, but make it a PERIODICALLY-NUDGED first-class backup (the matrix+nudge posture) so silent autosave loss is caught.

Add Crafting and Quests as separate TOP-LEVEL nav tabs (not nested panels). State the principle: the main screen stays the active labour/deeds/combat loop; any distinct non-critical activity gets its own top-level tab (still revealed one-at-a-time).

Replace any 'serialized one-per-beat queue' implication with: reveals are DESIGN-staggered one-at-a-time (no UI dumps), driven by the authored unlock schedule — no runtime queue. Each first-time reveal still pushes a diegetic log line.

Update 'text + emoji + CSS' to add: inline SVG for load-bearing period motifs (pillar/season/rarity marks, identical across OSes; emoji COSMETIC-ONLY) + a small curated audio set (synthesized Web Audio + original/CC0). Corrects the 'no asset pipeline' claim.

Add: while the tab is OPEN, auto-resolve combat + auto-repeat labour run unattended for hours (the 'leave it running, check progress' feel). Still strictly active-only — no offline catch-up; auto-producers stay T3+.
Add the mobile IA rule: per-tier PRIMARY tabs on the bottom bar (current-tier core), older tabs in an overflow drawer in reveal order.

Add (cross-ref ui-design.md): pillar bars show distance-to-next-gate; gain/loss number-flash uses the §2 gain/loss tokens; vermilion reserved for rank-up/seal beats; functional text uses --ink-soft (WCAG AA).

Scope the ARIA live region to narration + milestones ('polite'); add a persistent quiet a11y entry point from minute one; add a large-textScale reflow case + a screen-reader acceptance pass to the checklist.
Add a contrast bullet: functional/hint text uses --ink-soft (passes WCAG AA on every paper surface); --ink-faint is decorative-only; darken the meter fill for contrast.
Add gōshi/rōnin (and the 'Combat Rank' term) to the lang-tagging examples; reiterate the §6.6 lint flags untagged CJK.

Update the 'one RNG' row to per-named-stream cursors + day-keyed weather + Math.pow ban; the 'Active-only' row to add auto-resolve/auto-repeat; the 'Single source of truth' row to add the hardened verifier (gate-monotonicity, accrual tie-out, real-name lint, per-perk magnitude).

Rewrite: §6 now implements V2 decisions beyond D-013 — record the governing D-022 plus the specific new/changed locks it carries: multi-backend save (Q37), additive schema (Q45), RNG cursors (Q2), Math.pow ban (Q36), bounded cross-feed verifier flip (Q6), feature-rich data model incl. intra-line dialogue & subEngines (Q34), no-runtime-reveal-queue (FU4), asset-set correction (Q50/Q52/Q38). §6 is no longer 'elaboration only'.

Annotate the superseded resolutions (newest-wins, don't delete): item 1 IndexedDB single autosave -> SUPERSEDED by multi-backend (Q37); item 2 splitmix64+named sub-streams -> REFINED to persisted per-named-stream cursors (Q2); item 3 ordered forward migrations -> SUPERSEDED by additive-schema-first / migrations rare (Q45); item 4 tier stored -> KEEP.
Add an About/Credits surface (content/surfaces.ts) carrying authorship, the commit-SHA build stamp, font/audio attributions, and the license note; the build injects the commit SHA.

maps.notes:
Precedence applied throughout: Block M (FU) supersedes Block L (Q) supersedes A–K. Two FU overrides of Block-L Q's that the §6 author must honour: (1) FU4 KILLS Q17's 'deterministic one-per-beat reveal queue' — §6 must NOT add a stored revealQueue; reveals are design-staggered (s6-10, s6-23, s6-53). (2) FU8 makes the bounded cross-feed a PER-SKILL perk track with NO global cap and only a small-per-perk-magnitude verifier check — NOT the 'global <=CAP' some earlier proposals implied (s6-8, s6-28, s6-36).

Section-boundary ownership: §6 owns the DATA-MODEL SHAPE, the SAVE/RNG/DETERMINISM contracts, the registry SET, and the VERIFIER invariants. The actual NUMBERS (good/great/excellent bands, rung-meter thresholds, durability multipliers, satiety curve, weapon archetype params, win-rate bands, ~28.5h-floor budget) belong to §4 and live in content/balance.ts — §6 only stores their shape and proves their invariants. The combat-system narrative rewrite (FU12/FU13/FU14/Q15) and the hybrid-gate model (Q7/FU10) and sequential per-tier (FU7) are authored in §2/§3/§4; §6's job is to ensure GameState, the registries, and the verifier MATCH them — flagged as cross-section dependencies on s6-7/s6-9/s6-13/s6-16/s6-18/s6-21/s6-29/s6-30/s6-31/s6-36/s6-40 and the §6.4.1 new subsection.

ADR-worthy reversals concentrated in §6: multi-backend save (reverses 'IndexedDB single autosave', D-013/§6.13#1), additive schema (reverses 'ordered forward migrations', D-013a/§6.13#3), RNG cursors (refines 'splitmix64 named sub-streams', §6.13#2), Math.pow ban (new determinism lint), Date.now save-layer exemption (documented carve-out to the core no-wall-clock lint), the bounded cross-feed verifier flip (reverses the machine-checked '==0 no labour->combat' wall, D-011/D-016/D13 -> D-022), the asset-set correction (reverses K1 'no asset pipeline'). All sit under the governing D-022 (newest-wins).

M0/M1 build implications baked in: M0 ships the FULL multi-backend save layer (FU1) + the RNG cursor model (FU3) + a minimal stored skeleton {hp,satiety,attributePoints,character.level=1} (FU5) — subEngines/CombatEncounterState/dialogue are additive at M3/M5 (s6-22). Do NOT pre-declare speculative storage.

PRECEDENCE FLAG: the resolution-log (brainstorms/2026-06-26-prd-v2-followups.md) FU18 *proposed-lean* says budget is a CEILING; the SIGNED Block M FU18 reverses this to a FLOOR (newest-block-wins). This whole change-map follows the FLOOR. This is the single biggest §7 shift — it reframes §7.1.2, M6, and risk R2 from 'hit the budget exactly' to 'the grind is AT LEAST this long; only undershoot fails the gate.'

M0/M1 BUILDABLE SURFACE — confirmed/updated: M0 surface = {hp, satiety, attributePoints} + character.level(=1 floor) + satietyMax-at-floor (FU5/Q1/Q47); rng={seed,cursors:{combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen}} + stateless deriveDayKeyed weather (FU3); NO runtime reveal-queue, synchronous one-shot bus, design-staggered schedule (FU4); FULL multi-backend save layer (atomic write + magic field + monotonic-counter+timestamp newest-wins + additive schema), with ONLY the itch cross-origin-iframe survival TEST deferred to M7 (FU1/FU2); Math.pow banned in core (Q36). M1 surface = the numeric rung-meter (FU6, per-rung-reset, AND-gated with story) driving R0→R2, curated per-rung activities (FU7 phase 1), per-skill PERK field seeded on skills.ts (FU8, combat effect lands M2), tab-open auto-resolve+auto-repeat (FU23), fun-proxies report-only (Q4/FU9), mechanical ±10% weather (Q35). Both buildable.

STRUCTURAL RE-CUTS that need ADRs at integration: sequential phase-1/phase-2 per tier (FU7) restructures M1/M3/M4/M5 — most consequential to M3 (pillar deeds + four-bar panel + Arms-at-R6 move from interleaved R4–R7 into a post-R7 phase-2 sub-slice). Combat is re-spread M2a→M5 (FU12/FU13) — M2a's R3 'Lands' must shed the stance/ability/item/Equipment/Inventory/Bestiary UI-dump down to just weapon+loop+retreat. The no-cross-feed wall (Q6/FU8) and simple-threshold gate (Q7/FU10) reversals propagate into the M6 content-verifier invariant set.

NOT in my section but adjacent (flag for §2/§3/§4/§5 authors): the rung-meter law/curve (FU6) and hybrid-gate magnitudes (FU10) are §4; the three-track combat model rewrite (FU14) is §2.8; the combat-reveal-ladder row rewrites (FU12) are §2.7/§2.8/§3.2; the T3-stub header rewrite + name renames (Q24/Q27/Q39/Q11) are §5. §7 only references and must stay consistent with those.

maps.section: Cross-cutting NEW SYSTEMS (spanning §1, §2, §3, §4, §6) — the WHERE + shape

changes.location: Canonical home: NEW §1.6.4 + a §3 phase-2 stage per tier. Cross-ref rewrites: §1.6.3, §2.15(a), §2.16(e), §3.2/§3.4/§3.6 capstone rows, §4.1, §4.2.1, §4.8.1/.2/.3.
changes.what: SEQUENTIAL per-tier progression. Replace the current PARALLEL model (deeds accrue across all rungs; capstone rung 'confirms' a tier already gated by pillars) with a two-phase structure. PHASE 1: climb rungs R0->R7 (V0->V7, G0->G7) gated by the numeric rung-meter (cross-2) + story flags ONLY — pillar deeds do NOT accrue here. PHASE 2: clearing the final rung UNLOCKS the estate-influence/pillar grind ('really level up the estate'); grind the four pillars to the hybrid gate (cross-6) -> tier-up. Section touchpoints: NEW §1.6.4 states the model; §1.6.3 table 'Required pillars' column reframed as the phase-2 gate; §2.15(a) rewrite 'capstone confirms... pillars gated in parallel' -> 'capstone OPENS phase 2'; §2.16(e) rewrite 'pillars accrue from early deeds' -> 'pillar deeds accrue in phase 2 only'; §4.2.1 achievement JUMPS + §4.6.5 Arms-deed are phase-2-gated; §4.8 pacing tables split each tier's hours into phase-1 rung-climb + phase-2 grind; §3 each tier gains a phase-2 'estate-influence grind' stage after its capstone rung.

changes.location: Canonical home: NEW §4 subsection (e.g. §4.1bis 'The rung-meter accrual law'). Cross-ref rewrites: §2.15(a)/(c), §3.0 trigger taxonomy 'RANK' row, §3.2.1 spine, §4.8.1/.2/.3 'Estate Service x1/x2' notation, §6.6 verifier.
changes.what: The rung-meter accrual law (the missing third balance curve). Define a numeric meter: per-rung-RESET; each rung's threshold = (>=30-min floor x that rung's eligible curated-activity rate), back-solved the same way §4.8.1's koku column is; AND-gate (promote only when meter >= threshold AND all that rung's story flags set), with an 'awaiting X' read when one side lags. There are TWO such meters per tier — Estate Service (labour rungs) and Combat Rank (martial rungs, renamed per Q9, cross-11). Fuel = a CURATED per-rung activity set (1-to-many designed activities, NOT a single repeat-counter, NOT the §4.2.1 pillar-deed inventory). §2.15 'two earned standing meters' becomes this numeric law; §3.0 'RANK' trigger and §3.2.1 spine map each rung to (meter threshold AND story); §4.8 pacing tables replace the discrete 'Estate Service x1' marks with the meter target; §6.6 gains a gate-monotonicity check against the rung targets (Q28).

changes.location: Canonical home: NEW §4 subsection 'The three combat tracks + the character combat-level curve'. Rewrites: §2.8(a)/(c), §2.7(a)/(c)/(d), §4.4 (attr-points), §4.6.1 (hpMax), §4.6.5 (on-kill XP), §6.4 (character.level field).
changes.what: THREE clean combat tracks, de-conflated. Rewrite §2.8's 'character Combat Level = a Combat Deeds pool (combat-XP + deed-count of kills/clears/defends)' and delete the CombatDeedsPool data shape. The three independent stored tracks, one kill/deed writes to exactly one: (1) kills/combat-XP -> character.level (Q1, combat-fed ONLY; drives HP + attribute points); (2) recognized DEEDS -> the Arms pillar (§4.2.1, the influence economy); (3) per-rung curated activities -> the Combat-Rank rung-meter (cross-2). NEW §4 subsection defines the character combat-level XP->level curve (reuse §4.5.1 geometric shape, slower, fed by combat ONLY) so hpMax=40+8*characterLevel (§4.6.1), satietyMax-grows-with-level (Q47), and +1 attribute point / 2 levels (§4.4) all hang off ONE defined curve. mobLevel = explicit MobDef.level (FU15) is the on-kill XP source (§4.6.5, content/enemies.ts). Scope out / keep tiny the §2.7a 'compounding skill-XP multiplier' to avoid a combat->labour loop. Add character:{level} to the M0 stored surface (§6.4, FU5).

changes.location: Canonical home: NEW §3 'combat-reveal ladder' subsection + NEW §4.6 'weapon roster' subsection. Rewrites: §2.8(a)/(b)/(e), §2.7(e), §2.10(a)/(e), §3.2 R3 row + R4/R5 rows, §4.6.1/§4.6.2.
changes.what: Incremental combat-progression ladder + a ~9-10 weapon roster. Replace 'combat goes live at R3 dumping the whole system' and '2-3 weapon lines at T0' (§2.7e/§2.8/§3.2). NEW §3 combat-reveal ladder (one reveal per beat, FU12): R3 = ONE starter weapon + bare auto-resolve loop + retreat -> R4 = durability bands (with the loot->craft loop, cross-ref FU17) -> R5 = stance slot -> first weapon-line L10 milestone = ability/item slots -> new weapons revealed rung/tier-to-tier. NEW §4.6 roster subsection (FU13): T0 unlocks 2 weapons, T1 +3, T2 +4 (~9-10 across v1; T0 still STARTS with the one starter; crude carrying-pole = a 0th improvised weapon, not a line). Each weapon = archetype params (baseSpeed / reach / targetCount / attack profile) + a signature ability; §4.6.1/§4.6.2's single baseSpeed=1.0 becomes per-weapon. §2.10 weapon roster + staggered Equipment/Inventory reveal; §3.2 R3 row rewritten to the single-weapon beat.

changes.location: Canonical home: REWRITE §4.5.3 (-> per-skill perk tracks). Rewrites: §2.7(c)/(d), §4.4 no-cross-feed lines, §4.6.1 combatant model, §6.6 verifier, §4.9 levers/invariants.
changes.what: Per-skill perk system = bounded labour->combat cross-feed (Q6/FU8). Replace the §4.5.3 'no-cross-feed wall' (labour-skill+combat-attribute = type error) and the §6.6 '== 0 cross-feed' assertion. EVERY skill (combat + labour) gets a perk/flat-bonus track (~2-8 perks OR ~3-8 small flat stat bonuses, unlocked by leveling it); perks STACK, NO hard global cap. The combat bonus enters via a NEW separate additive term (skillCombatBonus on effectiveStats, §4.6.1) — NOT an attribute (avoids the §4.4 edge), NOT character level (Q1). Bound = small per-perk magnitude + incremental skill unlock (skills reveal per rung/tier, can't front-load) + holistic scaling. §6.6 verifier flips from '== 0' to 'each perk magnitude is small'. Conditioning STAYS the zero-stat enablement gate (don't let perks bypass it). Remove the 'no labour->combat cross-feed' HARD INVARIANT line from §4.9; annotate (don't delete) the prior canon.

changes.location: Canonical home: REWRITE §4.1 (canonical). Cross-ref rewrites: §1.6.3, §2.16(a)-gating-block + TierGate data shape, §4.2.1 (re-derive deed inventory), §4.8 tie-out, §6.6 verifier, §4.9 invariants.
changes.what: Hybrid good/great/excellent tier-gate (Q7/FU10), reversing 'simple thresholds, NO floor/overflow' (D-016/F3) — annotate prior canon, don't delete. §4.1 rewritten as the canonical home: per-pillar-per-tier good/great/excellent triples (good = expected baseline, great = really strong, excellent = above-and-beyond); distribution = good in ALL revealed pillars, great in 2-3, excellent in 1-2; T0 2-pillar special ('good in both + excellent in 1', no phantom 3rd); NO overflow-substitution. The triples need a DEDICATED rebalance against the fixed §4.2.1 deed inventory (NOT simple 0.2x/0.5x ratios). Per-tier REVEALED-pillar set: T0=2 (Arms+Estate), T1=3, T2=3 — must match the §3 reveal schedule + §2.16(e) panel reveal. §1.6.3 / §2.16 gating blocks rewritten from 'SIMPLE THRESHOLDS' to the hybrid distribution; TierGate data shape changes from Partial<Record<pillar,threshold>> to good/great/excellent bands; remove 'no floor/overflow fields exist'. This hybrid gate IS the phase-2 tier-gate (cross-1).

changes.location: Canonical home: REWRITE §6.8 + NEW §6.8.1 (backend architecture). Rewrites: §6.7 (RNG), §6.4 (RngState/character.level/save-meta/magic), §6.3 (dtTicks), §6.1 (Math.pow lint), §6.5 (registries), §6.6 (verifier).
changes.what: Multi-backend save layer + RNG cursors + determinism bans (Q37/FU1-5/Q2/Q3/Q36/Q44/Q45/Q46). §6.8 rewrite: redundant write to IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage; atomic write (Q44, never clear-then-rewrite) + calm 'export a backup' notice; app-identity magic field (Q46) with reject-to-recovery; newest-wins on load = monotonic save-COUNTER (real selector) + save-layer timestamp (tiebreaker, a documented core-lint exemption per FU2); backwards-compatible ADDITIVE schema (Q45, migrations rare) + raw-backup/rollback safety net; never load a forward-version backend. NEW §6.8.1 = the backend-abstraction architecture (built in full at M0, FU1). §6.7 rewrite: rng = { seed, cursors:{combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen} } (FU3/Q2) replacing { seed, counter }; delete 'split child RNGs from parent counter'; add a stateless deriveDayKeyed(seed,'weather',day) helper (NOT a persisted cursor, FU3/Q3). §6.4: RngState shape, character.level (FU5), saveCounter+saveTimestamp+app magic as metadata; do NOT pre-declare subEngines/CombatEncounterState/dialogue (Q45, additive at their milestone). §6.3: dtTicks whole-integer (remainder app-side). §6.1: ban Math.pow/exp/log/trig -> integer-pow + lint (Q36). §6.5: add content/dialogue.ts (FU22), content/world.ts (Q55), content/beliefBeasts.ts + festivals (Q3/Q55). §6.6: real-name lint + accrual tie-out + gate-monotonicity (Q28).

Intra-line dialogue branching (Q34/FU22). §2.12(c) TextLine gains a flat choices[] (+ ChoiceId); each choice's effect = locksLineIds[] / flagsSet. Data not scripting; deterministic (no RNG); save-light (only chosen-flags persist in GameState.flags, §6.4 — no new heavy storage; add the choices shape additively to content/dialogue.ts at M5, FU5/Q45). §6.3 advance_dialogue intent already carries choiceId? — keep-as-is, flag it as load-bearing for FU22.

Design-staggered reveals — NO runtime reveal-queue (FU4), resolving the conflict where the Q30/Q17 master-log said 'simultaneous reveals serialize into a deterministic one-per-beat queue'. FU4 supersedes (newest-wins): the unlock SCHEDULE is designed so reveals are inherently one-at-a-time (staggered by construction); genuine multi-element single-feature reveals are bespoke one-offs designed per case. §2.1 must NOT add a revealQueue[] to GameState; §3.0's fractal-incrementality rule stays but is framed as a design discipline, not a queue mechanism; reveal cadence is a gameplay/UI design responsibility. (Also reinforces NO-UI-DUMPS, Q17, across §3's R3 combat reveal per cross-4.)

Budget is a FLOOR, not a ceiling (FU18) + tab-open auto-resolve/auto-repeat (FU23). Reframe §4.8 from a fixed-total pacing model to a MINIMUM-grind model: the ~28.5h (T0 4.5 / T1 8 / T2 16) becomes a floor; the game can be a longer OSRS-rough grind (interleave, don't brick-wall). §4.8.0/§4.8.4 'LOCKED budget' -> 'LOCKED minimum'; §4.9's LOCKED-list and §7.1.2 acceptance criterion annotated (don't delete; D-016 annotated). v1 stays active-only (FU23): tab-open idle auto-resolve combat + auto-repeating labour give the 'leave it running, check progress' feel — §2.8 'auto-continue toggle' and §2.6 labour loop carry this; auto-producers stay T3+. This interacts with cross-1: each tier's floor splits into phase-1 rung-climb + phase-2 grind.

Terminology unification (Q9): rename the combat rung-gate 'Combat Standing' -> 'Combat Rank' project-wide, so 'Standing' refers only to the 官威 Standing-&-Office pillar. The Combat-Rank rung-meter (cross-2 / FU14 track 3) must NOT be reconflated with the Arms pillar (track 2) or with character combat-level (track 1). Macronize goshi/ronin per Q9. Keep the three combat tracks lexically distinct everywhere: 'character (combat) level' (track 1), 'Arms pillar' (track 2), 'Combat Rank meter' (track 3).

maps.notes:
CROSS-SECTION CONSISTENCY RISKS (shared numbers/terms that MUST agree across the rewrite):

1. CHARACTER COMBAT-LEVEL CURVE — defined ONCE (new §4 subsection, cross-3) and cross-referenced by: hpMax=40+8*characterLevel (§4.6.1), satietyMax-grows-with-level (Q47; §4.4/§2.3), +1 attribute point / 2 levels (§4.4), and on-kill XP=mobLevel*COMBAT_XP_K (§4.6.5). mobLevel must exist as MobDef.level (FU15) in §2.9 + content/enemies.ts (§6.5). If any of these re-derives its own level concept, combat power silently couples.

2. RUNG-METER vs PACING vs VERIFIER — the rung-meter thresholds (new §4.1bis) must be back-solved from the SAME >=30-min floor that §4.8 pacing uses, and asserted by the §6.6 gate-monotonicity check (Q28). The §4.8.1/.2/.3 'Estate Service x1/x2' discrete marks and the §3.0 'RANK' trigger must BOTH be re-expressed as the numeric meter + story AND-gate (cross-2). Don't leave the old discrete-crossing notation anywhere.

3. TWO-PHASE SPLIT (FU7) reshapes §4.8 — each tier's hour FLOOR (FU18, now a minimum not a target) splits into phase-1 rung-climb (rung-meter currency) + phase-2 pillar grind (the §4.2.1 deed inventory). The entire §4.2.1 deed inventory (T0 560 ip Estate / 350 Arms, etc.) MOVES to phase 2. §4.0 bands, §1.6.3, §2.16(e) 'pillars accrue from early deeds', and the §3 capstone 'confirms' rows must ALL agree deeds are phase-2-only and the capstone OPENS phase 2.

4. HYBRID GATE BANDS (FU10) must be re-derived against the FIXED §4.2.1 deed inventory and tie out in §4.8 + the §6.6 accrual-tie-out verifier (Q28). The per-tier REVEALED-pillar set (T0=2 Arms+Estate, T1=3, T2=3) must match the §3 reveal schedule AND the §2.16(e) four-bar-panel reveal. A mismatch reopens the 'good in ALL pillars' check against a pillar that isn't revealed yet.

5. WEAPON ROSTER — the count (~9-10: T0 2 / T1 +3 / T2 +4, FU13) and per-weapon params (baseSpeed/reach/targetCount + signature ability) must be byte-identical across §2.8/§2.10/§3 reveal rows/§4.6/content/items.ts. §4.6.1/§4.6.2's single baseSpeed=1.0 MUST become per-weapon, or the auto-battler math contradicts the roster.

6. RNG SHAPE — { seed, cursors:{combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen} } (FU3/Q2) must be identical in §6.3/§6.4/§6.7; purge { seed, counter } and 'split child RNGs from parent counter' everywhere. deriveDayKeyed(seed,'weather',day) is a STATELESS helper, NOT a persisted cursor — §6.4 must NOT store weather (only market-saturation persists, Q3).

7. SAVE SELECTOR — monotonic save-COUNTER is the real selector; save-layer timestamp is only the tiebreaker (a documented core-lint exemption, FU2); plus the app magic field (Q46). These three must agree across §6.4 stored metadata, §6.8 selector, and the §6.1 lint-exemption note. The deterministic core stays clock-free.

8. THREE COMBAT TRACKS stay lexically + mechanically distinct (Q9 rename, cross-11): 'character (combat) level' (track 1, kills/XP), 'Arms pillar' (track 2, deeds), 'Combat Rank meter' (track 3, curated rung activities). 'Combat Standing' -> 'Combat Rank' project-wide; 'Standing' = only the 官威 pillar. Reconflating any two is the single most likely regression given the old §2.8 'Combat Level = a Combat Deeds pool' text fused all three.

9. REVEAL STAGGERING — FU4 (NO runtime queue; design-staggered) SUPERSEDES the Q30/Q17 master-log phrasing 'serialize into a deterministic one-per-beat queue'. §2.1 must NOT add a revealQueue[] to GameState (newest-wins per the precedence rule). Reveal cadence is a design discipline, not a stored mechanism.

10. CONDITIONING stays the ZERO-stat enablement gate across BOTH cross-3 and cross-5 — the new per-skill perk cross-feed (FU8) is a SEPARATE small additive term; it must not become a back-door that bypasses conditioning's weak->capable gate. Keep them orthogonal in §2.8/§4.5/§4.6.1.
